_doc: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for how each model is configured AND invoked per task. Scripts read this (via run_bench.py) so invocations are canonical - no ad-hoc flags, nothing forgotten. think/num_predict are the per-task knobs; params document the best sampling baked into configs/*.Modelfile. think values are exactly what --think= expects: false | true | low | high. 'thinking': controllable | none | forced (gpt-oss: cannot be disabled, only leveled). reasoning num_predict is high for thinking models (thinking eats budget; runaway-safe). speed always think=false (throughput convention, matches the energy table).

qwen36-best.notes: unsloth params (top_p 0.8). Code needs think=false or it gets lost; reasoning needs thinking ON (+2.0).

qwen-coder-best.notes: Dedicated coder, no thinking. Efficiency king (~61 tok/s).

gpt-oss-best.notes: Thinking HARDCODED (harmony) - cannot disable, only level. Code profile temp 0.3; reasoning wants temp 1.0 (needs a separate Modelfile variant). NEVER temp 0 (100% loops). speed uses think=low (its floor).

north-best.notes: Thinking model. Code profile top_p 0.8; reasoning wants top_p 0.95 (needs a separate Modelfile variant).

phi4-best.notes: temp 0.7 measured more stable than native higher temp on reasoning (range 4 -> 0). Best reasoner (5.33), worst code.

devstral-best.notes: Base is the benchmarked devstral-small-2:24b-fast (NOT public devstral:24b). Slow (~10 tok/s), strong reasoning.

unsloth-q4xl-best.notes: Qwen3.5 Q4_K_XL, nothink baked in (cannot enable thinking). Balanced code 6 + reasoning 5.0.

gemma-best.notes: Gemma 4 E4B (8B total / ~4.5B effective). Official temp 1.0 (top_k 64, top_p 0.95). Thinking off by default; ON helps PL reasoning (5.5 -> 6.0). Also the neutral cross-family judge.

deepseek-r1-best.notes: CRITICAL: num_ctx MUST be 8192 - the default 131072 = 54 GB = crash on 64 GB. Very slow (~4 tok/s). Dropped from the main fleet (slowest + no winning axis).
